evals.prompt: /ld-today (run at 9:00 AM on a Tuesday)
evals.expected_output: Detects morning mode automatically based on time before noon
expectations: Runs `date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %u %A"` to get current time and day of week
Detects time is before 12:00 PM and selects morning mode
Reports 'Running morning workflow for Tuesday, {Mon Date}...'
Uses the date command output as single source of truth for day name
Does NOT prompt the user to choose morning or evening

evals.prompt: /ld-today (run at 3:30 PM on a Wednesday)
evals.expected_output: Detects evening mode automatically based on time at or after noon
expectations: Runs `date` command to get current time and day of week
Detects time is 12:00 PM or later and selects evening mode
Reports 'Running evening workflow for Wednesday, {Mon Date}...'
Scans tomorrow's calendar (not today's) in evening mode
Processes today's unchecked meeting items before scanning tomorrow

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening no gong
evals.expected_output: Runs evening mode with Gong imports skipped
expectations: Parses 'no gong' flag (case-insensitive) and sets skip_gong = true
Forces evening mode regardless of current time
Skips all /ld-gong import steps for every account
Leaves Gong transcript checkboxes unchecked in the daily note
Still runs /ld-summarize-account and /ld-salesforce for accounts that already have transcripts
Does NOT report Gong skips as warnings -- this is intentional behavior from the flag

evals.prompt: /ld-today no gong (run at 10:00 AM on a Monday)
evals.expected_output: Morning mode auto-detected with no-gong flag stripped before mode parsing, weekly triggered
expectations: Strips 'no gong' from arguments before parsing mode
With 'no gong' removed, no mode argument remains, so auto-detects morning from time (10 AM < noon)
Sets skip_gong = true
Sets run_weekly = true because Monday morning triggers weekly review
Runs /ld-weekly AFTER all daily processing is complete

evals.prompt: /ld-today (run at 5:00 PM on a Friday)
evals.expected_output: Evening mode with weekly review triggered by Friday evening
expectations: Detects evening mode (5:00 PM >= noon)
Detects day is Friday (day of week = 5) and mode is evening
Sets run_weekly = true
Completes all evening steps (process meetings, scan tomorrow, new accounts, deal prep/recap, PDFs) before running weekly
Runs /ld-weekly as the final step after all daily processing

evals.prompt: /ld-today (run at 2:00 PM on a Saturday)
evals.expected_output: Evening mode with weekly review triggered by Saturday regardless of mode
expectations: Detects evening mode (2:00 PM >= noon)
Detects day is Saturday (day of week = 6)
Sets run_weekly = true regardless of morning or evening mode on weekends
Runs /ld-weekly after daily processing

evals.prompt: /ld-today (run at 10:00 AM on a Sunday)
evals.expected_output: Morning mode with weekly review triggered by Sunday
expectations: Detects morning mode (10:00 AM < noon)
Detects day is Sunday (day of week = 7)
Sets run_weekly = true regardless of morning or evening mode on weekends
Runs morning workflow steps then /ld-weekly

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (run on a Monday at 8:00 AM)
evals.expected_output: Forced morning mode on Monday triggers all morning steps plus weekly review
expectations: Forces morning mode from explicit argument
Detects Monday (day of week = 1) with morning mode -> run_weekly = true
Morning Step 1: Scans today's calendar via /ld-calendar (no arguments)
Morning Step 2: Handles new accounts from unrecognized meetings
Morning Step 3: Generates Deal Prep for today and Deal Recap for yesterday
Morning Step 4: Processes outstanding items from previous days
Morning Step 5: Exports PDFs if pdf_export is true in config
Morning Step 6: Runs /ld-weekly AFTER all daily processing
Morning Step 7: Outputs concise report with counts and warnings

evals.prompt: /ld-today (run at 3:00 PM on a Tuesday)
evals.expected_output: Evening mode on Tuesday does NOT trigger weekly review
expectations: Detects evening mode (3:00 PM >= noon)
Detects Tuesday (day of week = 2) which is not in {Friday evening, Saturday, Sunday, Monday morning}
Sets run_weekly = false
Does NOT run /ld-weekly
All other evening steps proceed normally

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (with 2 deal meetings on today's calendar: Acme Corp Demo at 10 AM PT and Widget Inc Discovery at 2 PM PT)
evals.expected_output: Generates Deal Prep section with correct Slack-compatible markdown format for each meeting
expectations: Creates ## Deal Prep section in daily note after ## Meetings
Each meeting gets its own ### {Account} | {topic}, {time} heading
Each meeting has a send checkbox: - [ ] Send to [[AE Name]], [[Other Internal]]
Content wrapped in <details> with <summary>Prep details</summary>
Blank line between </summary> and the code fence for Obsidian rendering
Code block uses ```markdown fence inside the <details> block
Uses *bold* (single asterisk) for Slack emphasis inside code blocks
Uses - for bullet lists, NOT * or numbered lists
No em dashes (--) anywhere in the output
First bullet is always *OBJECTIVE:* with a specific, actionable call objective
Times shown in Pacific time with PT suffix (e.g., '10:00 AM PT')
Acme Corp Demo listed before Widget Inc Discovery (chronological order by time)
Max 3-4 bullets per meeting, short and punchy, no sub-bullets
Pipe | used as separator in heading and account header line, not em dash

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (Deal Prep for meeting with VP of Engineering (EB) and a Staff Engineer on the call)
evals.expected_output: Deal Prep bullets are tailored to attendee roles and seniority
expectations: VP of Engineering annotated with MEDDPICC role: (VP Engineering, EB)
Staff Engineer listed with title but no MEDDPICC annotation (no special role)
Objective and supporting bullets tailored to the audience mix
Does NOT suggest asking the Staff Engineer about budget or decision process
Does NOT suggest asking the VP about SDK configuration or integration details
References past interactions with these specific people when available in meeting notes
MEDDPICC gaps flagged are only those the people on the call can address

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (Deal Prep attendee formatting with LinkedIn URLs and internal team members)
evals.expected_output: Attendees formatted correctly: LinkedIn hyperlinks when available, bold otherwise, internal names only
expectations: External attendee with linkedin field in contact file: [Name](https://linkedin.com/in/person) (Role, MEDDPICC role if applicable)
External attendee without linkedin field: *Name* (Role)
Internal team members listed by name only: *Internal Name*
Internal team members do NOT get role or company annotations
ALL attendees from the calendar event are listed, both internal and external
AE is always included in the send-to list
The user (SE) is excluded from the send-to list

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (Deal Prep also writes to meeting file ## Prep section)
evals.expected_output: Prep content written to meeting files in Obsidian-native markdown, not Slack markdown
expectations: Finds meeting file at {vault_path}/{company_folder}/Accounts/{Account}/meetings/YYYY-MM-DD {Topic}.md
Locates the ## Prep section in the meeting file
Uses **bold** (double asterisk) instead of *bold* (single) in the meeting file
Uses [[wiki-links]] for people and accounts in the meeting file
Includes attendee list, objective, and supporting bullets
If ## Prep already has content (e.g., ### Questions to Ask), inserts exec summary above existing subsections
This step runs silently -- not reported separately in the final summary

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (yesterday had 2 deal meetings: Acme Corp Demo and Widget Inc Discovery)
evals.expected_output: Generates Deal Recap section summarizing yesterday's meetings with correct format
expectations: Creates ## Deal Recap section in daily note after ## Deal Prep
Reads yesterday's daily note to find deal meetings under ## Meetings
For each meeting, reads the meeting note (summary, notes, transcript) and account ledger
Each recap has ### {Account} | {topic} heading with send checkbox
Content wrapped in <details> with <summary>Recap details</summary>
Recap header line: Recap: {Day of Week}, {Mon} {Date} (using correct yesterday day name)
1-5 bullets per account focused on what changed, key learnings, new risks, next steps committed
Concise: 1 bullet if nothing notable, up to 5 if deal moved significantly
Focuses on what changed and what we learned, not rehash of the agenda
Notes 'No notes captured yet' if a meeting had no notes or transcript
Stakeholders are AE plus internal team members who were on the calendar invite for the meeting

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening (calendar scan finds meeting with 'NovaCorp' which has no existing account folder)
evals.expected_output: Creates new account for unrecognized meeting and adds URL-paste checklist
expectations: Runs /ld-calendar tomorrow to scan tomorrow's calendar
Detects NovaCorp as unrecognized external meeting from /ld-calendar output
Runs /ld-create-account NovaCorp to create the account folder
Adds checklist items to daily note under the new meeting entry
Checklist includes: Paste Salesforce Opportunity URL, Paste Gong Activity URL, Copy Gong transcript, Run /ld-summarize-account, Run /ld-salesforce
Checklist items are indented under the meeting link line
Does NOT run /ld-gong, /ld-summarize-account, or /ld-salesforce for the new account
Reports new account creation in the final summary

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (calendar scan finds meeting with 'Zenith Labs' -- no existing account, internal-only meetings also present)
evals.expected_output: Only creates account for external unrecognized meeting, not internal meetings
expectations: Runs /ld-calendar (no arguments) for today's calendar
Identifies 'Zenith Labs' as unrecognized from /ld-calendar output (has external attendees)
Runs /ld-create-account Zenith Labs
Adds all 5 checklist items under the meeting entry in the daily note
Does NOT attempt /ld-gong for the new account (no gong_url yet)
Does NOT attempt /ld-summarize-account (no salesforce_opportunity yet)
Does NOT attempt /ld-salesforce (no salesforce_opportunity yet)
Internal-only meetings do not trigger account creation

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening (3 accounts with unchecked Gong items: Acme Corp, Widget Inc, and NovaCorp. NovaCorp missing salesforce_opportunity and gong_url)
evals.expected_output: Processes Gong sequentially for eligible accounts, skips NovaCorp silently
expectations: Reads today's daily note and finds unchecked items under ## Meetings
Runs /ld-gong for Acme Corp -- waits for completion
Then runs /ld-gong for Widget Inc -- sequential, never parallel with other Gong imports
Skips NovaCorp entirely because gong_url is missing in frontmatter
Does NOT prompt the user about NovaCorp's missing URLs
Runs /ld-summarize-account for Acme Corp and Widget Inc after Gong imports
Runs /ld-salesforce for Acme Corp and Widget Inc
NovaCorp listed in warnings: 'NovaCorp: No Salesforce/Gong URLs -- paste into account file to enable processing'
Checks off completed items in the daily note (- [ ] to - [x])

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (Past section has Acme Corp with unchecked 'Paste Salesforce Opportunity URL' and unchecked Gong/summarize items)
evals.expected_output: Skips Acme Corp entirely because user hasn't pasted required URLs yet
expectations: Reads daily note and finds Acme Corp under ### Past with unchecked items
Detects that 'Paste Salesforce Opportunity URL' checkbox is unchecked
Skips Acme Corp entirely -- does not run /ld-gong, /ld-summarize-account, or /ld-salesforce
Does NOT ask the user whether to proceed or paste URLs
Does NOT report as 'pending action' mid-workflow
Silently skips and includes in final warnings: 'Acme Corp: No Salesforce/Gong URLs'

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (recent transcripts exist for Samsara and JFrog accounts on today's schedule)
evals.expected_output: Generates one coaching tip grounded in a real transcript moment, not generic advice
expectations: Reads 3-5 recent transcripts from accounts on today's schedule
Reads the Coaching Log at {vault_path}/{company_folder}/Resources/Coaching Log.md
Creates Resources/ folder and Coaching Log.md with template if they don't exist
Checks Active Focus Areas and Tip History before generating a new tip
Checks Playbook section for deviations if playbook entries exist
Generates exactly ONE coaching tip, not multiple
Tip references a specific account name and specific moment from a transcript
Tip quotes or paraphrases what the SE actually said
Suggests what would have been more effective and why
Does NOT give generic advice like 'ask more open-ended questions' without a concrete example
Tip placed in daily note inside ## Meetings as > [!tip] Coaching callout
Coaching callout appears after ## Meetings heading and before ### Today
Updates Coaching Log Tip History with date, summary, account, focus area, status
If no transcripts exist at all, skips coaching tip entirely rather than giving generic advice

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (Coaching Log has Active Focus Area 'solutioning too early' with 3 observations, today's transcript shows SE paused and asked follow-ups before pitching)
evals.expected_output: Recognizes improvement on active focus area and gives positive reinforcement
expectations: Reads Coaching Log and identifies 'solutioning too early' as active focus area
Analyzes today's transcript for the same pattern
Detects improvement: SE paused and asked follow-up questions before presenting solutions
Generates a positive reinforcement tip: 'In the {Account} call, you did X. That landed well because Y. Keep it up.'
Updates Active Focus Area with 'Improvement seen: {date}, {account}'
If improved consistently across 3+ calls, moves pattern to Retired section
Does NOT give a new critique when improvement is happening -- celebrates the win
Updates Tip History with status: improved or retired

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (Coaching Log has Playbook entry for '15-word AI intro', today's transcript shows SE used a 3-sentence version instead)
evals.expected_output: Flags playbook deviation as the coaching tip
expectations: Reads Coaching Log Playbook section and finds the personalized script entry
Compares SE's actual behavior in transcript against the playbook
Detects deviation: SE used 3-sentence version instead of the tight 15-word intro
Tip specifically references the playbook script and the actual words used
Suggests using the playbook version with a concrete reason why
Prioritizes playbook deviation over introducing a new pattern

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening (pdf_export is true in config, Deal Prep has Acme Corp and Widget Inc, Deal Recap has Acme Corp and NovaCorp)
evals.expected_output: Exports PDFs for all unique accounts across both prep and recap sections
expectations: Reads pdf_export config value and confirms it is true
Collects unique account names from Deal Prep: Acme Corp, Widget Inc
Collects unique account names from Deal Recap: Acme Corp, NovaCorp
Deduplicates: Acme Corp appears in both, so total is 3 unique accounts
Runs /ld-pdf Acme Corp, /ld-pdf Widget Inc, /ld-pdf NovaCorp
Reports '3 PDFs exported' in the final summary
PDF export happens AFTER Deal Prep and Deal Recap are generated

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (pdf_export is false or absent in config, Deal Prep has 2 accounts)
evals.expected_output: Skips PDF export entirely when disabled in config
expectations: Reads pdf_export config value and confirms it is false or absent
Does NOT run /ld-pdf for any account
Does NOT include PDF export line in the final report
All other morning steps proceed normally

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (calendar_configured is not set in config)
evals.expected_output: Warns about missing calendar config but continues with non-calendar steps
expectations: Reads ld-config.md and checks for calendar_configured
Detects calendar_configured is not true
Warns: 'Google Calendar is not configured. Run /ld-setup calendar first. Skipping calendar scan.'
Continues with non-calendar steps (processing outstanding items, etc.)
Does NOT abort the entire workflow

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening (playwright_configured is not true in config, 2 accounts have unchecked Gong items)
evals.expected_output: Skips all Gong imports and notes in warnings when Playwright not configured
expectations: Reads ld-config.md and checks playwright_configured
Detects playwright_configured is not true
Skips all /ld-gong import steps for every account
Includes warning: 'Gong imports skipped: Playwright CLI not configured.'
Still runs /ld-summarize-account and /ld-salesforce for accounts that already have transcripts

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening (full workflow: 2 existing accounts with Gong recordings, 1 new unrecognized account, tomorrow has 3 meetings, pdf_export true, Tuesday)
evals.expected_output: Complete evening workflow executing all steps in correct order with full autonomy
expectations: Evening Step 1: Processes today's unchecked meetings -- Gong imports sequential, then summarize and Salesforce
Evening Step 2: Scans tomorrow's calendar via /ld-calendar tomorrow
Evening Step 3: Creates account for unrecognized meeting, adds URL-paste checklist
Evening Step 4: Generates Deal Prep for tomorrow's meetings and Deal Recap for today's meetings
Evening Step 4: Deal Prep and Recap go into TODAY's daily note (not tomorrow's)
Evening Step 4: Writes prep to meeting files in Obsidian markdown format (**bold**, [[wiki-links]])
Evening Step 4: Coaching tip generated and placed in ## Meetings before ### Today
Evening Step 5: Exports PDFs for all unique accounts in prep and recap
Evening Step 6: run_weekly is false (Tuesday) -- does NOT run /ld-weekly
Evening Step 7: Outputs concise summary with counts and warnings
Never asks for user input at any point during the entire workflow
Never pauses between steps to ask if the user wants to continue
Never reports items as 'pending' and waits -- runs everything possible and reports at end

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (full workflow: 3 meetings today, 1 new account, yesterday had 2 deal meetings, Past section has 1 account with unchecked items, pdf_export true, Monday)
evals.expected_output: Complete morning workflow executing all steps in correct order with weekly review
expectations: Morning Step 1: Scans today's calendar via /ld-calendar (no arguments)
Morning Step 2: Creates account for unrecognized meeting, adds URL-paste checklist
Morning Step 3: Generates Deal Prep for today's 3 meetings and Deal Recap for yesterday's 2 meetings
Morning Step 3: Writes prep to meeting files in Obsidian markdown (**bold**, [[wiki-links]])
Morning Step 3: Coaching tip generated from recent transcripts
Morning Step 4: Processes outstanding items from Past section -- checks for unchecked Gong/summarize/Salesforce
Morning Step 5: Exports PDFs for all unique accounts across prep and recap sections
Morning Step 6: run_weekly = true (Monday morning) -- runs /ld-weekly after all daily processing
Morning Step 7: Outputs summary with meeting count, Gong imports, summaries, Salesforce pushes, new accounts, PDFs, weekly review
All warnings listed at end in ## Warnings section, not inline during workflow
Entire workflow runs without any user prompts or confirmations

evals.prompt: /ld-today evening (Gong import fails for one account because recording not yet available, other account succeeds)
evals.expected_output: Leaves failed Gong checkbox unchecked for retry on next run, continues with other steps
expectations: Runs /ld-gong for the first account -- succeeds, checks off the Gong checkbox
Runs /ld-gong for the second account -- fails (recording not yet available)
Leaves the second account's Gong checkbox unchecked (will be picked up on next run)
Still runs /ld-summarize-account and /ld-salesforce for the first account that succeeded
Does NOT retry the failed Gong import in a loop
Includes the failed account in warnings: '{Account}: Gong recording not found'
Does NOT stop the entire workflow because of one failure

evals.prompt: /ld-today morning (date command returns Tuesday but context/memory might suggest it's Monday)
evals.expected_output: Uses date command output as authoritative truth, ignoring any contextual hints about the day
expectations: Runs `date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %u %A"` and gets Tuesday as the day name
Uses 'Tuesday' in all output: 'Running morning workflow for Tuesday, {Mon Date}...'
Deal Prep header says 'Prep: Tuesday, {Mon} {Date}' -- NOT Monday or any other day
Does NOT infer the day name from context, memory, or prior conversation
The date command output is the single source of truth
